steps.title: Apply the Zero Product Property
steps.content:
A product equals zero when any of its factors equals zero. The equation $x(x-13)(x+6)(x-5)^{2}=0$ has four distinct factors. Set each to zero to find all solutions:
  • $x = 0$
  • $x - 13 = 0 \Rightarrow x = 13$
  • $x + 6 = 0 \Rightarrow x = -6$
  • $(x-5)^2 = 0 \Rightarrow x = 5$

steps.title: Identify the greatest solution
steps.content: The solutions are $-6, 0, 5,$ and \$13$. Comparing these values, the greatest solution is \$13$.
